item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, you had my curiosity at forcing site-19 staff to compete in a baking competition…then I got to the part where a Foundation HR employee is responsible, and then you had my attention.

It's honestly a little worrying that some Foundation Personnel might try and use or create anomalies for their own personal gain. It's the start of a slippery slope, if you summon a humanoid something to motivate your co-workers, why not try and grab some pixies to tidy up your office. Heck, if it's that easy, why stop at your office? Let's take the stuff home!

TL;DR: Nice work that makes me think. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand HR has a rough job. I can't imagine any HR that would have a worse time, except maaaybe the GOC. That being said, that ending.

+1 for Ouch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entity acknowledged the resulting questioning from attendees as "showing initiative but lacking enthusiasm" before discussing what it learned on a six month long African safari.

This is possibly the most horrifying thing I have ever read.

(Horrifying as in "scary" not "bad")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See! I'm not crazy! I've been telling people for years months that Foundation HR is interesting and desparately needs stories about it!

Do they have to worry about how you calculate back-pay for someone trapped in a temporal anomaly?

Hope this inspires others to see the light and give us more stories about HR, RAISA, and janitorial services.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should have probably used a designation for 4624's interior, which is just a customizable pocket dimension. I was trying to find a way to avoid just saying things like "pocket dimension" or "pocket universe" frequently as I don't find those terms particularly clinical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this overall, but I really think you should use a -1 or -A for the pocket dimension. I mean, just look at these sentences here:

55 minutes of SCP-4624 speaking about the values of community togetherness it learned while backpacking in Kashmir and how they can apply it to the workplace follows. Meeting attendees are then pulled into SCP-4624 where they emerge in a recreation of the Bunker Hill park in Massachusetts

Is SCP-4624 the humanoid or the location? This has got to be consistent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the things I'm grateful for in life is that, in my twenty four years of civil service employment, I never had to participate in a team-building exercise. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sgt. Pepper fired a 40 caliber handgun into its head.

I've wanted to end staff meetings this way. Love the descriptions of his speeches, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The identity of PoI-4624 was discovered after connecting the excavation of the grave of Anthony Mullard, born 1995/02/20 and deceased 2007/08/12, to Junior Manager of Human Resources, Stephanie Mullard.

oh fuck :o

item.title: Author Post, with enthusiasm
item.content:encoded:
Wanted to do a funny skip about mandatory teambuilding exercises and the HR mandated distractions that other people who work in an office are probably all-too-familiar with. About halfway through I found myself questioning why I was laughing at these people, they really do care about their work and I'm was mocking them. That is the sort of place I wanted the ending/theme of this skip to make the reader feel.

Thanks to Oboebandgeek99 Crashington LoveTheVoid and gee0765 for giving crit on the draft of this!

iMaGe AtTrIbUtIoN
god that felt juvenile to type
